bead.title: fix(try): push_failed must not set cooldown — route to execution_failed path
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:272-283 sets a 24h cooldown
  (maxAttemptCooldown) on push_failed outcomes via OutcomePark.
  This violates TD-031 §5 invariant (sister bead amends spec to
  state this explicitly): "execute-loop-retry-after may be set only
  when retrying the same bead after time passes could plausibly
  succeed without human/spec/dependency changes."

  push_failed causes are NOT time-recheckable:
    - Pre-push test failure (e.g., a data race in executor.go)
      requires a code fix.
    - Branch protection rule violation requires operator action.
    - Auth token expiry requires operator action.
    - Pre-push hook misconfiguration requires operator action.

  Observed impact: 5+ beads sat in ~24h cooldown after a 1-hour
  data-race incident. Drain idled ~10h after the fix landed
  because cooldowns survived their cause. Operator manually
  cleared cooldowns to restart progress.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:272-283 (push_failed branch):
    returns OutcomePark with maxAttemptCooldown (24h).
  - This routes through SetExecutionCooldown, which sets
    execute-loop-retry-after to now+24h.
  - The bead drops out of ready queue for ~24h regardless of
    whether the underlying cause has been fixed.

PROPOSED FIX
  Replace the OutcomePark branch with the same OutcomeReported path
  used by execution_failed:
    - Status transition: in_progress → open (claim released).
    - Event: push-failed with stderr detail.
    - DO NOT set execute-loop-retry-after.
    - Bead becomes immediately eligible for next-pass claim.
    - If the underlying cause persists (test still fails), next
      attempt fails the same way and the post-attempt triage hook
      can route to status=proposed when retry budget is exhausted.

  Concretely:
    - cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:272-283: change the
      push_failed branch to construct OutcomeReported (not
      OutcomePark) with the same report.
    - Verify no callers depend on the cooldown side effect of
      OutcomePark for push_failed (grep for "push_failed" + "park"
      across cli/internal/agent/).
    - Update test cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go (or
      sibling): assert push_failed produces no
      execute-loop-retry-after.
    - Add positive test: TestPushFailed_NoCooldown asserts
      after a push_failed, the next ddx bead ready returns the
      bead immediately.

NON-SCOPE
  - declined_needs_decomposition cooldown (sister bead R4 fix).
  - Bulk clear-cooldowns command (sister bead R2).
  - Rev-bound auto-invalidation (sister bead R3).
  - Triage routing for genuine operator-required cases (post-attempt
    hook can already route to proposed; that path is unchanged).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(TD-031): codify push_failed/push_conflict
    outcome rows" must land first; this fix aligns with that spec
    row.
  - Sister bead R3 (rev-bound auto-invalidation) becomes simpler
    after this lands (one fewer bogus cooldown class).
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go push_failed branch (around line 272-283) returns OutcomeReported (or equivalent) instead of OutcomePark; no maxAttemptCooldown is applied.
2. After a push_failed outcome, the bead's Extra contains NO execute-loop-retry-after key.
3. TestPushFailed_NoCooldown in cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go asserts the bead is immediately re-claimable (appears in Store.Ready() output) after a push_failed.
4. Existing push_failed tests are updated to reflect the new no-cooldown semantics.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
